div.p: WHERE keeps rows only when the predicate is TRUE. Both FALSE and NULL cause the row to be discarded. This is why WHERE col = NULL returns 0 rows — use WHERE col IS NULL instead. The OP_IsNull guard prevents NULL operands from reaching comparison opcodes, ensuring rows with NULL columns are skipped before the comparison even fires.
Rule: FALSE wins over NULL FALSE AND <anything> = FALSE
Rule: TRUE wins over NULL TRUE OR <anything> = TRUE
Rule: NULL propagates NOT NULL = NULL

div.h2: WHERE val = NULL Result: 0 rows Every comparison with NULL yields NULL (not TRUE). WHERE keeps only TRUE rows.
div.p: 10 = NULL → NULL → discard NULL = NULL → NULL → discard 30 = NULL → NULL → discard

ul.li: If reg[p1].type == VAL_NULL → jump to jump_addr
If reg[p1].type != VAL_NULL → fall through to next instruction

p.strong: Compiled WHERE age > 21 with NULL guard:

div.p: addr opcode p1 p2 p3 comment

3 Column 0 1 r2 age → r2 4 Integer 21 0 r3 21 → r3 5 IsNull r2 8 0 if age NULL → skip 6 IsNull r3 8 0 if 21 NULL → skip 7 Le r2 8 r3 if age≤21 → skip 8 Column 0 0 r1 name → r1 (output) 9 ResultRow r1 1 0 emit row 10 Next 0 3 0 advance cursor
